skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
stem: At a university, 400 students were selected at random and surveyed about a proposed change to the academic calendar. All 400 students completed the survey. It was estimated that 42% of the students were in support of the change, with a margin of error of 4 percentage points. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the survey results?

answerOptions.content: The percent of all students at the university who support the change is exactly 42%.
Plausible values of the percent of all students at the university who support the change are between 38% and 46%.
Plausible values of the number of all students at the university who support the change are between 38 and 46 students.
The percent of all students at the university who support the change is at most 42%.

rationale:
Choice B is correct. With an estimate of 42% and a margin of error of 4 percentage points, the plausible values for the population percent range from 38% to 46%.

Choice A is incorrect: a margin of error indicates a range, not a single exact value.

Choice C is incorrect: the units are percents, not students.

Choice D is incorrect: the margin of error gives both a lower and an upper bound.
